2 min read
Just now

--

Press enter or click to view image in full size

Dylan Field questioned something bigger..
Why were designers still emailing files back and forth..
Saving endless versions..
Waiting for feedback..
Working alone..
Design wasn’t the bottleneck..
Collaboration was..
Most design tools were built for individuals..
One file..
One computer..
One editor at a time..
That worked..
Until teams became global..
Remote..
And moving faster than ever..
So he built Figma..
A browser-first design platform..
Where everyone could work together..
At the same time..
Designers..
Developers..
Product managers..
Marketers..
One shared canvas..
Live collaboration..
Instant feedback..
No version chaos..
No “final_v27_real_final.fig”..
Then AI entered the workflow..
Not to replace designers..
But to remove repetitive work..
Generate layouts..
Speed up iterations..
Automate routine tasks..
So creators could spend more time making decisions..
Instead of moving pixels..
That’s the real advantage..
AI isn’t the product..
It’s the accelerator..
And the platform became stronger with every team that adopted it..
Design systems expanded..
Templates spread..
Workflows became standardized..
The ecosystem compounded..
Because Figma wasn’t just selling design software..
It was building the operating system for product design..
Here’s what Dylan Field saw before everyone else..
1.. Collaboration beats individual productivity
The fastest teams outperform the fastest individuals..
2.. Remove friction, and creativity increases
People create more when the process gets out of the way..
3.. AI should amplify expertise, not replace it
The best tools help humans think faster — not think less..
Lesson:
Don’t just build a better tool..
Build a place where people can create together..
Because when collaboration becomes effortless..
Innovation scales naturally..
That’s exactly what I amplify..
Solving real audio related problems in your business..
👉 santelmomusic.com
